title: Firebase sync: UI integration — sync status indicator and settings (#154 pt 3)
body:
Part of #154

SCOPE: Wire the sync hook into the app shell, show a sync status indicator, and add a Firebase credentials setup section in Settings so users can enter their own Firebase project config.

FILES:
- src/components/SyncStatusBadge.tsx (new) — small badge showing synced/syncing/error
- src/components/Settings.tsx (existing) — add Firebase config input fields
- src/App.tsx or main shell (existing) — call useFirebaseSync() on mount

DEPENDENCIES: Depends on sub-issues for Firebase utilities and sync service.

NOTES: Settings UI should accept apiKey, projectId, appId inputs and persist them to localStorage. Show a "Copy setup instructions" helper. The sync hook reads credentials from localStorage before initializing Firebase.

TESTING: Unit test that SyncStatusBadge renders correct state for each sync status value. Integration test for settings persistence.
enhanced_description:
Firebase sync UI integration: wire sync hook into app shell, add sync status badge, and add Firebase credentials setup in Settings (Part 3 of #154).

What was requested:
- Create SyncStatusBadge.tsx component that visually indicates sync state (synced / syncing / error)
- Add Firebase credentials input section to Settings.tsx (apiKey, projectId, appId fields persisted to localStorage)
- Call useFirebaseSync() hook in App.tsx (or main shell) on mount to activate sync
- Show a "Copy setup instructions" helper in settings

Affected components:
- src/components/SyncStatusBadge.tsx (NEW) — small badge component, reads syncStatus from useFirebaseSync hook
- src/components/Settings.tsx (EXISTING) — add Firebase config section with apiKey/projectId/appId inputs, localStorage persistence, and copy-instructions helper
- src/App.tsx or main shell (EXISTING) — call useFirebaseSync() to activate sync on mount; pass status to badge

Dependencies:
- Depends on #157 (firebaseConfig.ts, firestoreUtils.ts) — already implemented
- Depends on #158 (useFirebaseSync hook, syncService.ts) — already implemented
- No new npm packages needed (firebase already installed)

Expected behavior:
- User opens Settings, sees a Firebase Sync section with fields for apiKey, projectId, and appId
- User pastes their Firebase project credentials; values are saved to localStorage
- On next load, the sync hook reads credentials from localStorage and initializes Firebase
- SyncStatusBadge renders in the app shell showing current sync state (idle/syncing/synced/error)
- "Copy setup instructions" button provides a help text snippet explaining how to get Firebase credentials

Current behavior:
- No sync UI exists; useFirebaseSync hook from #158 is implemented but not wired into the app

Edge cases to handle:
- Empty/invalid credentials: show validation error, do not attempt to init Firebase
- Sync error state: badge shows error icon with tooltip or small message
- Settings fields should not expose credentials in plain text (use type=password or obscure by default)
- If credentials are cleared from settings, reset Firebase instance (call resetFirebaseConfig)
- SyncStatusBadge should gracefully render nothing (or idle state) when Firebase is not configured

Acceptance criteria:
- SyncStatusBadge is visible in app shell and reflects real-time syncStatus from useFirebaseSync
- Settings.tsx has a Firebase Sync section where credentials can be entered and saved to localStorage
- App.tsx calls useFirebaseSync() on mount; credentials from localStorage are used to init Firebase
- Removing credentials disables sync (graceful no-op)
- Component renders correctly for all 4 sync states: idle, syncing, synced, error

Testing:
- Unit test SyncStatusBadge: renders correct icon/text for each of the 4 sync status values (idle, syncing, synced, error)
- Unit test Settings Firebase section: entering and saving credentials persists to localStorage; clearing credentials removes them
- Unit test App.tsx integration: useFirebaseSync is called on mount; syncStatus flows to badge
- Test edge case: SyncStatusBadge with undefined/null status renders gracefully

title: [Deploy Failed] Build failure on 457f6b0
body:
**Deployment failed** (auto-created by pipeline)

**Commit:** [`457f6b0`](https://github.com/akashkguw/babybloom/commit/457f6b0)
**Time:** 2026-03-29 16:47 PDT
**Actions run:** https://github.com/akashkguw/babybloom/actions/runs/23721887385

## Failure Details

**Job:** test (20) — FAILED
**Failed step:** Install dependencies

## Recent Commits

```
457f6b0 Auto-deploy: update BabyBloom — closes #157 closes #158
4a373af Implement: Firebase sync: core IndexedDB ↔ Firestore bidirectional sync service (closes #158)
c5c4a1c Auto-deploy: update BabyBloom — closes #157
826595e Implement: Firebase setup: install SDK and create config/Firestore utilities (closes #157)
0a51267 Auto-deploy: update BabyBloom — closes #156
```

## Next Steps

1. Check the [failed Actions run](https://github.com/akashkguw/babybloom/actions/runs/23721887385) for full logs
2. Fix the issue locally and push
3. Pipeline will auto-close this issue on next successful deploy

---
_Auto-created by BabyBloom pipeline on deploy failure._
enhanced_description:
GitHub Actions CI failed on commit 457f6b0 at the "Install dependencies" step (job: test (20)).

What was reported:
- The automated deploy pipeline triggered a GitHub Actions run after deploying Firebase SDK and sync service (issues #157 and #158)
- The CI job "test (20)" failed at the "Install dependencies" step — npm/package install could not complete

Affected area:
- GitHub Actions CI workflow (not src/ code)
- Likely .github/workflows/*.yml or package.json/package-lock.json

Root cause hypothesis:
- Firebase SDK packages added in #157 may have introduced a dependency conflict, peer dependency warning treated as error, or an incompatible package version
- Alternatively the CI node/npm version may be incompatible with a newly added Firebase dependency
- The package-lock.json generated on macOS may have platform-specific entries that fail in the Linux CI runner

Expected behaviour:
- npm install (or equivalent) completes successfully in CI
- All downstream test, build, and smoke test steps pass

Current behaviour:
- "Install dependencies" step fails, blocking the entire CI run and any subsequent deploy

Edge cases to handle:
- Check if package-lock.json needs to be regenerated or if engines field in package.json restricts node version
- Verify no macOS-only binary dependencies were locked into package-lock.json
- Check if Firebase packages have peerDependency conflicts with existing packages

Acceptance criteria:
- GitHub Actions "Install dependencies" step passes on the latest commit
- All subsequent CI steps (unit tests, TypeScript build, smoke test) also pass
- No new CI failures introduced

Testing:
- Verify locally that npm ci (clean install) succeeds from a fresh node_modules state
- Check the Actions run logs for the exact npm error message to identify the specific package conflict
- After fix, confirm CI run passes end-to-end on GitHub Actions
implementation_notes:
WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENED:
- Root cause: After Firebase SDK was installed (commits #157, #158), the package-lock.json was regenerated but the @testing-library/jest-dom and @testing-library/react entries (and their transitive deps) were dropped from the lockfile
- The lockfile was regenerated locally (npm install) which restored the missing entries, but the updated lockfile was never committed
- CI runs npm ci which requires exact lockfile/package.json sync — it fails with: npm error Missing: @testing-library/jest-dom@6.9.1 from lock file

Files changed:
- package-lock.json: committed the locally-regenerated version that includes @testing-library/jest-dom, @testing-library/react and all their transitive dependencies (224 lines added)

Approach taken:
- Confirmed the local npm ci succeeds (adds 715 packages)
- Verified type-check passes
- Confirmed the settings-ui.test.ts test failure is local-only (due to uncommitted working-tree changes from failed issue #159) — committed Settings.tsx has Data & Support, so CI tests pass

Testing done:
- npm ci: SUCCESS (adds 715 packages, no errors)
- tsc --noEmit: CLEAN (0 errors)
- Local unit tests: 535/536 pass (1 failure is in settings-ui.test.ts which reads the working-tree Settings.tsx modified by failed issue #159 — unrelated to this fix)

Manual steps needed: None — CI will pass automatically after this commit
